channel.title: Aniki Bulletin
channel.description: Important notices, tips, feature spotlights and community news for Aniki ReMake and Aniki Helper

item.title: Feature Spotlight – New Splash Screen system
item.description:
The Splash Screen feature has been improved in Aniki Helper.

This system is designed to make game launches feel closer to a real console experience inside Playnite Fullscreen.

Splash images and videos are configured in Desktop mode. You can manage them either from the Aniki Helper plugin settings, or by right-clicking a game and selecting Aniki Helper → Manage Splash Screen.

  • Video splash screens are now supported.
  • A new management window has been added for easier setup.
  • Multiple splash files can be added for the same game.
  • If several files are available, Aniki Helper can randomly select one.
  • A splash file can be marked as the main splash with a star, so it will always be used instead of random selection.
  • Users can switch back to random selection at any time.
  • Splash screens can be managed per game, globally, by source, or by platform.
  • Several priority modes are available.
  • The automatic priority mode uses: custom game splash → Playnite background → source → platform → global.
  • Game logos can be displayed on image splash screens.
  • Logo position can be customized: left, center, right, top, middle, or bottom.
  • Video splash screens can play with or without sound.
  • Video volume can be adjusted directly from the plugin settings.
  • When a video splash ends before the minimum display timer, users can choose whether to show the game background or keep the last frame of the video until the timer finishes.
  • A global minimum display timer slider has been added.
  • A custom minimum display timer can also be set per game.
  • Splash files are now organized into clean folders for Global, Sources, Platforms, and Games.

If you want a cleaner and more cinematic launch experience, this is one of the main features to configure.

item.title: Important Notice – ThemeOptions menu may close unexpectedly
item.description:
A controller navigation issue was found in Aniki Helper v1.3.5.

When navigating inside the ThemeOptions menu, opening some ComboBox options could accidentally close the entire ThemeOptions window when pressing D-Pad Up.

This could happen with options such as Theme Color, Avatar, and other settings that use ComboBox menus.

  • The issue was introduced with Aniki Helper v1.3.5.
  • The issue has been fixed in Aniki Helper v1.3.6.
  • If your ThemeOptions menu closes unexpectedly when using D-Pad Up, please update Aniki Helper.

This is especially recommended for users who customize the theme directly from Playnite Fullscreen with a controller.

item.title: Quick Guide – Where to configure splash images and videos
item.description:
Splash images and videos are configured from Playnite Desktop mode.

There are two main ways to manage splash screens:

  • Open the Aniki Helper plugin settings to configure global, source, platform and default splash behavior.
  • Right-click a game, then choose Aniki Helper → Manage Splash Screen to configure splash files for that specific game.

Per-game splash screens are useful for games you are currently playing, while global, source and platform splash screens are better for creating a consistent style across your library.

For the most console-like result, you can combine a custom splash image or video with the game logo display option.

item.title: Feature Spotlight – Aniki notifications and Playnite integration
item.description:
Aniki Helper now handles more theme-related systems directly.

The goal is to improve the overall Aniki ReMake experience and make Playnite Fullscreen feel more consistent when using custom views, panels and controller navigation.

  • Aniki Helper can keep a dedicated Aniki notification history.
  • Theme and plugin notifications can be separated from default Playnite notifications.
  • Game session notifications can be displayed after closing a game.
  • Steam update notifications can be shown when updates are detected.
  • The What's New window can be shown only when needed after a theme update.
  • Custom Aniki views can open some Playnite menus more reliably.
  • Custom windows and child overlays have better controller navigation behavior.
  • Fullscreen menus are styled more consistently with the Aniki interface.

These systems are part of what makes Aniki Helper important for the full Aniki ReMake experience.

item.title: Interface Highlight – What's New, Quick Access and Steam news
item.description:
Aniki ReMake continues to move toward a more complete console-like interface for Playnite Fullscreen.

Recent interface improvements focus on easier access to important views, better update communication, and more useful game detail pages.

  • A What's New view can show important update information directly inside the theme.
  • The avatar can open a Quick Access menu.
  • The Quick Access menu gives access to views, power options, notifications, help, resources and update information.
  • An Aniki Helper tab is available inside Playnite's settings menu.
  • A warning message can appear when Aniki Helper is outdated.
  • The game details view can show Steam news for the selected game.
  • The previous per-game update button has been replaced by a News button.
  • The game details page can show up to 8 latest Steam news entries.
  • An option is available to hide the central top bar buttons.
  • Controller mapping has been improved so the same button can open and close the Hub.

The goal is to make Playnite on PC feel less like a launcher and more like a dedicated living-room console interface.

item.title: Community Highlight – New avatars, filters and Iris color theme
item.description:
Some recent Aniki ReMake additions were made possible thanks to the community.

  • New avatar assets have been added.
  • New filter images have been added.
  • A new Iris color theme has been added.

Thanks to the users who contributed assets, ideas and feedback for the theme.

Community contributions help make Aniki ReMake more complete, more personal and more fun to use.

item.title: Recommended Setup – Optional add-ons for the full Aniki experience
item.description:
Aniki ReMake can be used with only the required add-ons, but several optional add-ons are supported for a richer experience.

  • PlayniteAchievements is recommended if you want achievements directly inside Playnite Fullscreen.
  • Steam Friends Fullscreen can display your Steam friends presence inside the fullscreen interface.
  • NowPlaying helps you quickly return to or exit currently running games from the Playnite interface.
  • BackgroundChanger can randomize covers and wallpapers for a more dynamic library.
  • Screenshot Visualizer can display your screenshots and video captures.
  • Screenshot Utilities can display screenshots from different stores.
  • HowLongToBeat can show estimated completion times for your games.
  • UniPlaySong is recommended for background music and per-game music if you want a more console-like audio experience.
  • Friends Achievement Feed is recommended to display a feed of achievements from your friends.
  • Friends Achievement Feed – Fullscreen Companion displays the Friends Achievement Feed plugin in fullscreen mode.
  • Custom Fade Animations is recommended to make background image transitions smoother and more pleasant.

You do not need to install every optional add-on. Install only the features you actually want to use.

item.title: Performance Tip – Recommended settings to reduce stuttering
item.description:
If Aniki ReMake feels laggy or stutters while navigating, check these settings first.

Most stuttering issues are usually caused by heavy media loading, video streaming, animated backgrounds, logo effects, blur effects, GPU drivers, or VRR behavior.

Even if your PC can play 4K videos easily, rendering trailers or animated wallpapers inside a fullscreen WPF interface is different. Playnite has to render the video together with the full UI, animations, overlays and background effects, which can be heavier on some systems, especially laptops or older GPU drivers.

  • In ExtraMetadataLoader, disable video streaming and prefer downloaded trailers instead.
  • If navigation feels slower, try disabling trailers or animated background wallpapers in ThemeOptions to check if they are causing the stutter.
  • Animated backgrounds, video trailers on main view, and Dynamic Colors can increase GPU/CPU usage and may create slowdowns or small stutters on some setups, even if the video resolution is low.
  • Update your GPU driver if you notice performance drops, stuttering, or unusual slowdown in Playnite Fullscreen.
  • In ExtraMetadataLoader → Logo Settings, disable logo shadow effects and opacity animations if navigation feels slow.
  • In Playnite Fullscreen, set Blur Background to OFF. Blur is expensive in Playnite's WPF interface.
  • Use the Balanced scaling mode in Playnite Fullscreen settings.
  • Enable Asynchronous image loading in Playnite Fullscreen settings.
  • Disable Smooth Scrolling if you want more responsive navigation.
  • On laptops, make sure Playnite is using the dedicated GPU instead of the integrated graphics.
  • If you use G-Sync, FreeSync or VRR, try disabling it specifically for Playnite.

If disabling videos or animated wallpapers makes GPU usage drop back to normal or improves navigation, the issue is likely related to video rendering, animations, visual effects, or your GPU driver rather than a memory leak.

These settings can make a noticeable difference, especially on laptops, older GPU drivers, lower-end PCs, or very large libraries with many high-resolution images, trailers and animated backgrounds.

item.title: Recommended Audio Setup – Music and event sounds
item.description:
For the best audio experience with Aniki ReMake, use the right tool for the right purpose.

  • UniPlaySong is recommended for background music, per-game music, audio normalization, fade transitions and a more console-like music experience.
  • Aniki Helper includes its own event sounds feature for theme-related actions and fullscreen feedback.
  • If you enable event sounds in Aniki Helper, it is recommended to disable UI/event sounds in Playnite Sound Mod to avoid duplicated audio feedback.
  • If you prefer using Playnite Sound Mod for event sounds, keep Aniki Helper event sounds disabled.
  • To avoid audio conflicts with videos, disable the Pause music during video trailer option in Playnite Sound Mod.

The goal is to avoid overlapping audio systems and keep the fullscreen experience clean, consistent and free from duplicate sound effects.

item.title: Security Reminder – Be careful with unofficial Playnite downloads and sponsorship emails
item.description:
Be careful with emails or websites pretending to represent Playnite.

Some users have reported emails offering paid promotion for Playnite. These messages should be treated as suspicious unless they come from an official and verified source.

Only download Playnite from the official website or the official GitHub page.

  • Do not trust unofficial Playnite installers from random websites.
  • Do not trust sponsorship emails claiming to represent Playnite without verification.
  • When in doubt, check the official Playnite sources first.

This warning is included here because it can affect Playnite users in general, not only Aniki users.
